title: caucalis infesta subsp. heterophylla
intro: Caucalis infesta subsp. heterophylla, also known as the "Wild Carrot" or "Queen Anne's Lace," is a flowering plant in the carrot family (Apiaceae). It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to stimulate digestion, reduce inflammation, and act as a diuretic. The health benefits of Caucalis infesta subsp. heterophylla include its potential to reduce the risk of heart disease, improve digestion, and alleviate symptoms of anxiety and insomnia. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
